payload.contentString:
Topic: MyChart - Other

I'm trying to send a message to my care team but I get the following errors:

> Messaging is available to MyUnityPoint users after they have a scheduled appointment with a MyUnityPoint provider. After you have scheduled an appointment, your profile will be linked to the provider's care team and messaging will be available.

I have already completed an appointment with Dr. Dhillon, so it looks like something may be misconfigured?

Thanks for helping me get this turned on!

Josh

topic.text: RE: "Message Your Care Team" not working

payload.contentString:
Hi Joshua,

I've sent your request to message Dr. Dhillon to our Advance Support to review.  These may take a few days for a response.  Please contact your provider via phone if needing assistance before you hear back from me.

Sincerely,
Gayla
MyUnityPoint Technical Support

For additional support, please feel free to contact the MyUnityPoint support team by calling 877-224-4430 Monday - Friday 8am to 5pm.

Josh

Josh,
I understand that you have questions about the criteria for the MRI vs CT of the head.  I called AIM Specialty Health which is the company your insurance carrier uses for prior authorizations of diagnostic imaging.  They cannot fax me a copy of the criteria but directed me to the clinical guidelines on their website.  I would advise you to look at the criteria for MRI of the brain and CT of the head on their website.  Please remember that the CT was approved, but MRI was not approved.  Please let us know what you want to do.
Thank you,
Mary Ann Picone, DNP

After reviewing the trade-offs, I would like to proceed with scheduling a self-pay MRI at Midwest Open MRI in Middleton, rather than pursuing a head CT. Can you please help me get an order in place for the MRI study?

Thanks,
Josh

topic.text: RE:  Dr. Dhillon departure from Associated Physicians

payload.contentString:
January 21, 2021

 Re: Primary Care Physician Assignment

 Dear Valued Patient:

 I have made the difficult decision to leave my medical practice at Associated Physicians, to focus on my family. I have enjoyed my time at Associated Physicians and being your primary care provider.

 I understand that you may have concerns or questions about this transition. Associated Physicians is actively recruiting a new Internal Medicine physician to become your new primary care physician. In the interim, all plans of care will continue without disruption as one of my Internal Medicine partners will ensure your healthcare needs are met. We are developing plans to address outstanding appointments, insurance issues and billing. If you have an appointment scheduled after my departure date, one of our staff will reach out to reschedule. I know Associated Physicians will continue to provide excellent care to you and your family.

 Yours very truly,

 Puneet S. Dhillon M.D.

Appointment Request From: Joshua C Mandel

 With Provider: Mary A Picone, DNP [Assoc Physicians Internal Medicine]

 Preferred Date Range: 4/5/2021  4/9/2021

 Preferred Times: Any Time

 Reason for visit: COVID-19 Vaccine appointment

 Comments:
 COVID-19 Vaccine appointment

We are not scheduling random COVID injections. The schedulers are following a list of those available and will be calling patient's as their name comes up. We also encourage patients to check with the pharmacies as sometimes you will be able to get in with them sooner.

thanks for getting back to me. Can you let me know where my name is on the list and what the algorithm is for choosing eligible members of the practice, if it is not a random algorithm? Thanks,

 Josh absolutely do

Hello, Josh!
 I apologize in the delay in our response. Our providers have made the decisions regarding vaccination lists keeping in mind the limited availability of doses. Initially, we vaccinated patients based on age. Now that DHS has opened eligibility to all residents aged 16 and over, the providers have run a report/list of the patients who are most at risk given the medical conditions for the 1c group. Unfortunately, we are also at the mercy of the supply given each week which has been significantly less than we request. I would be happy to send a message to our clinical staff to review your specific situation. You are welcome to either reply here, send a MyChart message or call the office at 608-233-9746 to have a message sent back to the clinical team. Thank you for your understanding!

 Alyssa, CTR
Thanks! It looks like my doctor has retired from the practice, but between overweight and prehypertension I'm hoping I'll qualify for vaccine ASAP.

PLEASE DO NOT RESPOND TO THIS MESSAGE

We currently have a supply of the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine. We are able to vaccinate anyone 16 years and older who are established patients of Associated Physicians (seen a provider within the past 3 years). Appointments will be subject to supply and will only be scheduled one week at a time. Please note, anyone under the age of 18 years old, must be accompanied by a parent or guardian (no exceptions can be made). To schedule an appointment, please call the clinic number, (608) 233-9746, and a reception staff member will be happy to assist you in scheduling the appointment. We look forward to seeing you!

Please disregard if you have already received a COVID-19 vaccine.

Dear Valued Patient of Associated Physicians: We hope this letter finds you well. Associated Physicians is excited to welcome Zoe Rammelkamp, M.D. Dr. Rammelkamp is joining our practice following her residency at the University of Minnesota School of Medicine. Dr. Rammelkamp will begin her practice at the end of August 2021 at Associated Physicians.
You are receiving this announcement because our records indicate that you have Dr. Puneet Dhillon listed as your primary care physician (pcp). We have greatly appreciated your patience during our search for a new physician. We are confident that Dr. Rammelkamp will continue to provide you the excellent care you have come to expect from Associated Physicians. Dr. Rammelkamp is excited to meet you and learn about your health needs. Please call the clinic at (608) 233-9746 to schedule an appointment if you have not already scheduled a visit with Dr. Rammelkamp.
Sincerely,
The Physicians of Associated Physicians, LLP

*Please disregard this letter if you plan to establish care with one of our other providers, your pcp will be updated after you meet with that provider*

Joshua C Mandel would like to cancel the following appointments:

 MAC LAB APL in MAC APL LABORATORY (1700801005), 10/11/2022 3:30 PM

Hi Dr. Rammelkamp,

 When I saw you in August, we discussed my ongoing challenges with post
 -concussive symptoms (subsequent to a mild TBI in 2020). My overall trajectory had been going in the right direction, but (alas!) I've managed to re-injure my head on vacation earlier this month. This has been a significant setback (I'm experiencing headache, "brain fog", mild nausea -- all worse with reading / intensive visual activity).

 I have a PPO health plan and don't need a formal referral, but I wanted to check if there's a local neurologist that you'd recommend (ideally someone with experience managing concussion or post concussive syndrome). I'd appreciate any leads!

Josh,
Dr Zoe has the following respond to your question:
"I Don't have any specific recommendations. It is hard to be seen by Neurology so she usually recommends seeing whoever has availability the soonest. You may need to wait a few months to be seen.
I have multiple patients who like Dr Lake, Neurology. I don't think she specializes in concussions but she does work with headaches." Dr Zoe
Let us know if you have any more questions.
Teresa G RN

topic.text: Associated Physicians No Show Policy Update for All Patients

payload.contentString:
Josh,

 We appreciate you choosing Associated Physicians, LLP. We schedule our appointments so each patient receives the right amount of time to be seen by our providers and staff. That is why it is very important that you keep your scheduled appointment with us and arrive 15 minutes early to complete the check-in process.
 As a courtesy, we attempt to provide each patient with an appointment reminder (via text, phone, or email) to confirm their appointment one week prior to the appointment. A second appointment reminder is sent the night before the appointment. To facilitate this communication, we ask that you update us if any of your contact information is changed.

If your schedule changes, and you cannot keep your appointment, please contact us at (608) 233-9746 to reschedule your appointment as soon as possible; this allows us to accommodate patients who are waiting for an appointment. As a courtesy to our office, as well as the other patients who are waiting to schedule with our physicians, please call at least 24 hours before your scheduled appointment to cancel.

 If you do not come to your appointment, we may assess a $20 "no-show" service charge to your account. This "no-show" service charge is not reimbursable by your insurance company. You will be billed directly.

 If you do not come to your new patient/establish care visit, you may reschedule one more time with AP; however, you will not receive priority over other new patients that are already scheduled.
 We understand that unforeseen emergencies occasionally happen. If one of these events occurs prior to your appointment, please call the clinic at (608) 233-9746 to inform our staff as soon as you are aware of the impact on your appointment. The fee may be waived for a single event.
 After three consecutive no-shows to your appointment, our practice may terminate its relationship with you.

 While most patients will never see this fee, it is important that everyone understands the importance of attending all your appointments, and communicating with us in a timely manner if your schedule changes.

 We appreciate your understanding regarding this change to our practice. We enjoy caring for you and your family members.

The Providers and Staff at Associated Physicians

August 3, 2023

 Dear Mr. Mandel:

 Life is busy. We understand going to the doctor isnt always top-of-mind. But thats why were here, to help you stay on top of your health and wellness.

 We noticed youre due for the following:

 Annual Wellness Visit

 Weve made it easy to view your preventive care reminders and request an appointment with your provider  all in one click epichttp://healthmaint[here]. If youve already received this service, send us a short MyUnityPoint message, and well update your records.

 Keeping you healthy and happy is our top priority, and we look forward to hearing from you soon.

 Sincerely,
 Your UnityPoint Health Care Team

Appointment Request From: Joshua C Mandel

 With Provider: Zoe L Rammelkamp, MD [Assoc Physicians Internal Medicine]

 Preferred Date Range: 8/23/2023  8/24/2023

 Preferred Times: Any Time

 Reason for visit: Pain, skin abrasion over sacrum after injury

 Comments:
 Pain, inflamed skin over sacrum/coccyx

Hi Josh,
 We received an appointment request, can you tell me more about what you are experiencing and when it started?

 Any drainage or fever?

 Karen, RN

(Looks like my message got stuck in draft, sorry!)

 Sure! 5 days ago I jumped backwards into a metal rail, striking my lower back. I scraped the skin over my lower spine / tailbone; sitting has been uncomfortable, and I can't tell if the skin is healing well.

Hi Josh,
 It looks like you were able to view my message, but I do not see a response. Are you still in need of an appointment?

 Karen, RN

Okay, looking again this morning I can see things are going in the right direction. Healing is for sure progressing compared to a few days ago. I think I'm fine without an appointment at this stage -- thanks again for the response here.

Hi Josh,
 No problem, glad it is healing! Definitely let us know if you do not continue to see improvement.

 Karen, RN

(This is more a technical curiosity than an actual concern! Feel free to just shrug.)

 The weight scale at my visit read 180.0 (with shoes on etc), but the recorded weight in Epic says 82.5 kg (181 lb 12.8 oz).

 I only noticed this because of course it trips me over the overweight BMI threshold of 25 ;-)

 I'm not sure where the disconnect would be in data capture.

If your saying you saw 180 on the scale and could have just been a typo. I can update your weight to 180 lbs.

 Megan
Yes, that's correct, thanks!

Refills have been requested for the following medications:

 nortriptyline (PAMELOR) 10 MG capsule [Dr. Z Rammelkamp]
 Patient Comment: Can you please update this to 30 mg daily and a 3 month supply? This will match the prescription from neurologist consult.

 Preferred pharmacy: Other - Costco in Middleton
 Delivery method: Pickup

Hi Dr. Rammelkamp,

 I'm very interested in learning more about how my body responds to different foods and how that affects my blood sugar levels -- and to this end I'd like to try a continuous glucose monitor. Since the new OTC Dexcom Stelo won't be available for a few months, could you possibly write me a prescription for the Abbott FreeStyle Libre 3?

 Thanks!
 -Josh

Josh,
 Have you checked with your insurance to see if they will pay for this? It is likely that they will not cover one since you do not have Diabetes. They are Quite expensive.

 Please let us know after you check with insurance. Thank you.

 Teresa G RN
Thanks -- I'm okay paying out of pocket if my insurance won't cover.

Hi Josh, what pharmacy would you like them sent to?
As we are unable to provide a medical diagnosis to indicate these supplies you will have to pay of pocket. Let me know the pharmacy and if you still want me to ask pcp.
 Kathleen
Thanks -- the Costco in Middleton would be great.

topic.text: PCP Out of Office July 1 - July 14

payload.contentString:
Hello all-

 I hope that everyone is doing well. I am writing to notify you that I will be out of the country for two weeks (from July 1- July 14). I will not be checking patient medical charts during this time, so there will be some lab results that come through that I will not see until I am back in Madison. Additionally, if there are non-urgent questions that come up during those two weeks, then I will not be able to see those questions until I return, so I recommend either holding on to them, or just being aware of the fact that I will not be replying until July 15th. My colleagues will be able to reply to all urgent items, and we have a fantastic nurse practitioner who can see you for urgent care matters (cough, sore throat, fevers, pain, etc.). I apologize for the inconvenience, but I did want to set expectations with all of you before I leave the country.

 Sincerely,
 Zoe Rammelkamp, MD

I'm having a new focal pain in my neck since Sunday evening, worse with swallowing or direct pressure. It's concerning me given the location over my left carotid artery (worry spiraling to: "what if this is an early sign of carotid dissection").

 Is there any chance Dr. Rammelkamp has availability for an appointment today?

Josh,

 Dr Rammelkamp is not in Clinic on Tuesdays.

 Would you like an appointment in the near Future to have this evaluated?

 Teresa G RN

Thanks for the quick reply. How soon would it likely be?

 (I'm considering whether to go to urgent care because I'd like to figure this out as soon as reasonably possible.)

Josh,

 She does have an opening on Thursday at 11:30. Does that date and time work for you?

 Teresa G RN

Josh,

 The Thursday appointment is at 2:45. Are you available?

 Teresa G RN

 Your response has been received.

Hi Dr. Rammelkamp,

 Would you be able to order specific IgE blood tests for peanut and tree nut allergies? As a child, I experienced frequent hives, and skin testing by an allergist indicated allergies to peanuts and several tree nuts, including almonds, walnuts, hazelnuts, pecans, cashews, pistachios, and macadamia nuts. I have avoided peanuts and tree nuts since then, but I'd like to assess whether these allergies may have resolved.

 Best,
 Josh
Hi Josh- I don't think that we have that lab here. I am happy to refer you to Allergy Clinic and they can do the allergy testing. Let me know if interested. Thanks.

Thanks for the quick response! These are tests that LabCorp could do with an order for Test 604764 (CPT 86003(x9), details at https://www.labcorp.com/tests/604764/allergen-profile-nut-ige-with-component-reflexes). I'd love to start with this before pursuing an allergy clinic consultation, if you're comfortable with that.

Josh- I'm sorry, but I would feel more comfortable if you had an allergist go through these things. I have literally never ordered these tests, nor sent them to LabCorp, so it is not in my comfort zone. Let me know if you want the referral though.
Okay. If you think there's any chance they could get me in by the end of the year then I would be happy to have a referral.

Josh,

 I am not sure if you can get in by the end of the year, that depends on their schedule. We can try though and see what they do. Let us know if you do not hear from UW allergy about scheduling by late next week.

 Karen, RN

Hello Josh C Mandel,
 Your medication refill request has been received. Please allow at least 2 business days for refills to be sent to your pharmacy.

 Please note that some medications require prior authorization, and it can take 3-5 business days to receive a response from your insurance company. If your medication requires a printed prescription, we will notify you when it is ready to be picked up at your clinic.

 We encourage all patients to request refills at least 7 days in advance of needing them so we can help ensure your medication supply is not interrupted.

 Thank you for choosing UnityPoint Health.

 Karen, RN

Dear Joshua C Mandel,
 From your care team at Associated Physicians, we’d like to wish you a very Happy Birthday! We encourage you to take some time around your birthday to focus on your health. And, because we care so much about your ongoing wellness, we see this as a great time to remind you of any preventive screenings, tests and vaccines that may be due. According to our records, we suggest you receive the following:

 Health Maintenance Topics Due in Your Medical Record
 Topic

 Hepatitis B Vaccine (1 of 3 - 19+ 3-dose series)

 Human Papillomavirus Vaccine (HPV) (1 - 3-dose SCDM series)

We recommend that you address your health maintenance items above by either:
Discussing the items with your care team at your next regular office visit.
Requesting an appointment via MyChart or by calling us at 608-233-9746.
Calling us or sending a MyChart message for further clarification.Your health care is important to us, and we want to ensure that we have the most accurate information possible. Please send us a MyChart message or call us at 608-233-9746 if the health information we have above is incorrect or you have received these services at another location. We would be happy to update your record.
 We recommend you contact your insurance company to verify which of these preventive services are covered. For example, Medicare does NOT currently cover the shingles vaccine or under certain circumstance, the Tdap vaccine and Hepatitis C screening.
 Best wishes to you in the upcoming year,
Your Care Team at Associated Physicians

topic.text: Dr. Rammelkamp out of the office

payload.contentString:
Hello everyone-
 I hope that you are all enjoying the summer. I am writing to notify my patientsthat I will be out of the country, and without access to Epic, from August 2ndthrough August 13th. I will be back in the country and able to respond to messages/ result notes/ refill requests on August 14th. I will be back in the clinic on August 15th. If you have a question specifically for me, then please know that I will not be able to respond until mid-August. I recommend holding on to those questions until I return, if possible. If something urgent comes up, then please rest assured that one of my colleagues will take good care of you.

 Let me know if questions about any of this! I appreciate your help. Oh, and I am also sending to Stef in case she is able to help with this, too.

 Best,
 Zoe Rammelkamp, MD

I apologize for the multiple messages that have been sent. - Steffany Louko

topic.text: Worth repeating a fasting lipid panel?

payload.contentString: I think it's probably worth repeating this fasting.

Hi Josh- overall these labs are fine. The triglycerides are elevated, which is likely related to not fasting. The LDL (bad cholesterol) is fine, as is the total cholesterol. The A1C (diabetes screen) is fine. If you would to come back fasting for the lipid panel, then let us know and someone can schedule a morning lab visit for you. Or, if you would like to wait till your next annual wellness visit, then that is fine; I would just recommend fasting for that visit.

December 15, 2025

Joshua C Mandel
 [REDACTED-ADDRESS-1]
 [REDACTED-ADDRESS-2]
Dear Valued Patient,

 I have made the difficult decision to leave my medical practice at Associated Physicians in order to pursue a career in clinical research. I have enjoyed my time at Associated Physicians and being your primary care provider. I will continue to see patients through March 5, 2026.

 I understand that you may have concerns or questions about this transition. Associated Physicians is actively recruiting a new physician to serve as my replacement, and an announcement will be made when the new physician is ready to schedule. In the meantime, my colleagues will address your acute concerns and medication refill requests. If you have an appointment scheduled after our departure date, one of our staff will reach out to reschedule.

 We appreciate your patience while we work to reschedule already scheduled visits and coordinate your preventative care. If you are unable to wait for the next available provider or if you prefer to transition your care to a different clinic, our staff will assist you in transferring records to your new clinic.

 I wish all of you the best. It has been an honor to serve as your doctor.

 Yours very truly,

 Zoe Rammelkamp, MD

Topic: Question/Other.

 Since Dr. Rammelkamp is leaving the practice I wanted to see if I could switch over to Dr. Fothergill. Thanks so much, Josh

Hi Josh,

 Please call the clinic at 608-233-9746 to discuss the options in regards to establishing care with one of the physicians. Dr. Fothergill is accepting new patients so scheduling with her would be an option for you.

 Thanks
 Jess
I did call and I was told that I could make a selection and share a message here. Could we continue on this channel?

Hi Josh,

I am sending you this message to your Mychart as the original message you sent did not go through your Mychart but instead was sent to "Mychart Administration" and then was forwarded to us from that department.

 I just wanted to let you know that I did try to call you in regards to scheduling an appointment with Dr. Fothergill but had to leave a message. Since I was unable to reach by phone, I went ahead and scheduled an appointment for you with Dr. Fothergill on the following date: 6/16/27 2:30 pm
Future AppointmentsDateTimeProviderDepartmentCenter6/16/2027 2:30 PMFothergill, Amy, MDAPLIMAssoc PhysAs you can see this appointment is scheduled for June of 2027. This is Dr. Fothergill's first available transfer of care appointment.
 If you need to reschedule, it is important that you call the clinic to do so. Our phone number if 608-233-9746. There are other options that are available with other physicians if you do not want to wait until 2027 and it is best that these options are discussed with a telephone conversation.

 Let me know if you have any questions.

Thanks
